    22→│   ├── inference/            # Model loading and inference factories
    23→│   ├── model-training/       # Fine-tuning and training pipelines

    44→**libs/data-pipeline/**

    49→
    50→**libs/evaluation/**
    51→- Purpose: Offline benchmarks, metrics, and performance evaluation
    52→- Contains: Evaluation harnesses, metric computation

    54→- Used by: Model validation, performance tracking
    55→
    56→**libs/events-py/**

   128→
   129→## Key File Locations
   130→
   131→**Entry Points:**
   132→- API Service: `services/api-service/src/api_service/main.py` - FastAPI app instance
   133→- Agent A: `services/agent-a-service/src/agent_a_service/graph.py` - `get_graph()` function
   134→- Agent B: `services/agent-b-service/src/agent_b_service/graph.py` - `get_graph()` function
   135→- Frontend: `apps/hitl-ui/src/` - React app entry (typically `main.tsx` or similar)
   136→
   137→**Configuration:**
   138→- Root workspace: `pyproject.toml` - UV workspace members, pytest settings, mypy config, coverage settings
   139→- API Service: `services/api-service/pyproject.toml` - Service-specific dependencies
   140→- Agent Services: `services/agent-{a,b}-service/pyproject.toml` - Service dependencies
   141→- Libraries: `libs/*/pyproject.toml` - Library-specific dependencies
   142→- Database migrations: `services/api-service/alembic/` - SQLAlchemy schema versioning
   143→
   144→**Core Logic:**
   145→- Shared models: `libs/shared/src/shared/models.py` - `Entity`, `Task` base classes
   146→- Lazy loading: `libs/shared/src/shared/lazy_cache.py` - `@lazy_singleton` decorator
   147→- Inference: `libs/inference/src/inference/factory.py` - `create_structured_extractor()` with Pydantic validation
   148→- Node implementations: `services/agent-{a,b}-service/src/agent_*/nodes.py` - Workflow logic
   149→- Database access: `services/api-service/src/api_service/dependencies.py` - `get_db()` context manager
   150→
   151→**Testing:**
   152→- Python tests: Located in `services/*/tests/` and `libs/*/tests/` directories
   153→- Frontend tests: `apps/hitl-ui/src/test/` (units and examples)
   154→- E2E tests: `apps/hitl-ui/e2e/` (Playwright)
   155→- Test config: Configured in root `pyproject.toml` [tool.pytest.ini_options]
   156→
   157→**Shared Utilities:**
   158→- Event handling: `libs/events-py/src/events_py/models.py` - `create_event()` function
   159→- Prompt templates: `libs/shared/src/shared/templates/` - Jinja2 files referenced by inference factory
   160→- Template access: `libs/shared/src/shared/__init__.py` - `get_prompts_dir()` function
   161→
   162→## Naming Conventions
   163→
   164→**Files:**
   165→- Python modules: `snake_case.py` (e.g., `lazy_cache.py`, `main.py`, `dependencies.py`)
   166→- Configuration: `pyproject.toml`, `alembic.ini`, `.env` variants
   167→- Documentation: `README.md`, `PROJECT_OVERVIEW.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`
   168→- Test files: `test_*.py` or `*_test.py` (pytest discovery)
   169→
   170→**Directories:**
   171→- Services: `kebab-case` (e.g., `api-service`, `agent-a-service`)
   172→- Libraries: `kebab-case` (e.g., `data-pipeline`, `model-training`)
   173→- Python packages: `snake_case` (e.g., `api_service`, `agent_a_service`)
   174→- Source code: Always under `src/` directory for proper packaging
   175→- Frontend: React convention for components and hooks
   176→
   177→**Functions:**
   178→- Async node functions: `noun_verb` (e.g., `extraction_node`, `validation_node`)
   179→- Getter/factory functions: `get_*` (e.g., `get_db()`, `get_graph()`, `get_llm()`)
   180→- Creator functions: `create_*` (e.g., `create_db_and_tables()`, `create_graph()`, `create_structured_extractor()`)
   181→
   182→**Classes:**
   183→- Pydantic models: `PascalCase` (e.g., `Entity`, `Task`, `AgentState`)
   184→- Enum classes: `PascalCase` (e.g., `EventKind`)
   185→- Exception classes: `PascalCase` ending in `Error` (e.g., `AgentError`, `AgentTimeoutError`)
   186→
   187→**Types:**
   188→- TypedDict: `PascalCase` (e.g., `AgentState`, `EventEnvelope`)
   189→- Type variables: `TModel`, `T` (generic conventions)
   190→
   191→## Where to Add New Code
   192→
   193→**New Feature (business logic):**
   194→- Primary code: `services/api-service/src/api_service/` for orchestration logic
   195→- Agent-specific logic: `services/agent-{a,b}-service/src/agent_*/nodes.py` for node implementations
   196→- Shared models: `libs/shared/src/shared/models.py` for data structures
   197→- Tests: Mirror source directory structure (e.g., `services/api-service/tests/`)
   198→
   199→**New Agent Workflow:**
   200→- Create new directory: `services/agent-name-service/`
   201→- Copy structure from `services/agent-a-service/`:
   202→  - `src/agent_name_service/state.py` - TypedDict state definition
   203→  - `src/agent_name_service/graph.py` - StateGraph with `create_graph()` and `get_graph()`
   204→  - `src/agent_name_service/nodes.py` - Async node functions
   205→  - `src/agent_name_service/__init__.py` - Package marker
   206→- Create `pyproject.toml` matching `services/agent-a-service/pyproject.toml`
   207→- Add to root `pyproject.toml` [tool.uv.workspace] members list
   208→- Add test directory: `src/agent_name_service/tests/`
   209→
   210→**New Utility/Shared Component:**
   211→- Utility function: `libs/shared/src/shared/` or new lib under `libs/`
   212→- Model class: `libs/shared/src/shared/models.py` or dedicated lib
   213→- Event type: `libs/events-py/src/events_py/models.py`
   214→- Prompt template: `libs/shared/src/shared/templates/` as Jinja2 file
   215→
   216→**New Microservice Library:**
   217→- Create directory: `libs/new-lib-name/`
   218→- Copy structure from `libs/inference/`:
   219→  - `src/new_lib_name/` - Implementation code
   220→  - `docs/` - Library documentation
   221→  - `tests/` - Unit tests
   222→  - `pyproject.toml` - Dependencies and metadata
   223→- Add to root `pyproject.toml` [tool.uv.workspace] members
   224→- Update root `pyproject.toml` pythonpath to include `libs/new-lib-name/src`
   225→
   226→**Frontend Component:**
   227→- Feature components: `apps/hitl-ui/src/features/feature-name/` (feature-first)
   228→- Reusable components: `apps/hitl-ui/src/components/` (by category)
   229→- Screen/page: `apps/hitl-ui/src/screens/screen-name.tsx`
   230→- Store/state: `apps/hitl-ui/src/stores/` (Pinia or similar)
   231→- Tests: Co-located with components or in `src/test/` directory
   232→
   233→**Database Migration:**
   234→- Create revision: `cd services/api-service && alembic revision --autogenerate -m "Description"`
   235→- Migration file: `services/api-service/alembic/versions/xxxx_description.py`
   236→- Models: Update `libs/shared/src/shared/models.py` to match schema
   237→
   238→## Special Directories
   239→
   240→**alembic/ (Database Migrations):**

   246→**docs/ (Documentation):**

   258→**node_modules/ (Frontend Dependencies):**

   263→
   264→**.uv_cache/ (UV Package Cache):**

   268→
   269→**.venv/ or venv/ (Virtual Environment):**

   274→**.planning/ (GSD Planning):**

   280→**infra/ (Infrastructure):**

1→# Testing Patterns
     2→
     3→**Analysis Date:** 2026-03-02
     4→
     5→## Test Framework
     6→
     7→**Runner:**
     8→- pytest 9.0.2+
     9→- Config: `pyproject.toml` lines 141-170
    10→- Async support: `pytest-asyncio` with `asyncio_mode = "auto"`
    11→
    12→**Assertion Library:**
    13→- Standard pytest assertions (e.g., `assert x == y`)
    14→- Exception testing: `pytest.raises(ExceptionType, match="regex")`
    15→
    16→**Run Commands:**
    17→```bash
    18→uv run pytest                    # Run all tests
    19→uv run pytest -n auto           # Run tests in parallel (default: pytest-xdist)
    20→uv run pytest --watch           # Watch mode
    21→uv run pytest --cov             # Coverage report (optional)
    22→```
    23→
    24→**Coverage:**
    25→- Optional: `pytest --cov` enables coverage
    26→- Configuration: `pyproject.toml` lines 172-194
    27→- Coverage sources: All `src/` directories across services and libs
    28→- Excluded: Test files, venv, virtual environments
    29→- Line exclusions: `pragma: no cover`, `__repr__`, `__main__`, `TYPE_CHECKING`
    30→
    31→## Test File Organization
    32→
    33→**Location:**
    34→- Tests are co-located in `tests/` directory alongside source
    35→- Pattern: `{service|lib}/tests/` at same level as `{service|lib}/src/`
    36→- Examples:
    37→  - `services/api-service/tests/`
    38→  - `libs/events-py/tests/`
    39→
    40→**Naming:**
    41→- Test files: `test_*.py` or `*_test.py`
    42→- Test classes: `Test{ComponentName}` (e.g., `TestCalculateTotalPrice`, `TestInventoryService`)
    43→- Test functions: `test_{functionality}` (e.g., `test_create_event_has_required_fields()`)
    44→
    45→**Structure:**
    46→```
    47→{service|lib}/
    48→├── src/
    49→│   └── {package}/
    50→│       ├── __init__.py
    51→│       ├── main.py
    52→│       └── ...
    53→└── tests/
    54→    ├── conftest.py          # Shared fixtures
    55→    ├── test_example_unit.py
    56→    ├── test_example_integration.py
    57→    └── test_example_mocking.py
    58→```
    59→
    60→## Test Structure
    61→
    62→**Suite Organization:**
    63→```python
    64→# From services/api-service/tests/test_example_unit.py
    65→
    66→class TestCalculateTotalPrice:
    67→    """Test suite for calculate_total_price function."""
    68→
    69→    def test_calculate_single_item(self):
    70→        """Test calculation with a single item."""
    71→        items = [{"price": 10.0, "quantity": 2}]
    72→        result = calculate_total_price(items)
    73→        assert result == 22.0  # (10 * 2) * 1.1
    74→
    75→    def test_empty_items_raises_error(self):
    76→        """Test that empty items list raises ValueError."""
    77→        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Items list cannot be empty"):
    78→            calculate_total_price([])
    79→
    80→    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
    81→        "items,tax_rate,expected",
    82→        [
    83→            ([{"price": 10, "quantity": 1}], 0.1, 11.0),
    84→            ([{"price": 20, "quantity": 2}], 0.15, 46.0),
    85→        ],
    86→    )
    87→    def test_parametrized_calculations(self, items, tax_rate, expected):
    88→        """Test multiple scenarios using parametrize."""
    89→        result = calculate_total_price(items, tax_rate)
    90→        assert result == expected
    91→```
    92→
    93→**Patterns:**
    94→- **Setup:** Use pytest fixtures from `conftest.py` for initialization
    95→- **Teardown:** Automatic via fixture generators (yield pattern)
    96→  - Example: `db_session` fixture rolls back after each test
    97→  - Example: `test_client` clears dependency overrides after test
    98→- **Assertion style:** Direct assertions with descriptive comments
    99→  - `assert result == expected_value  # calculated value matches`
   100→
   101→## Mocking
   102→
   103→**Framework:**
   104→- `unittest.mock` (built-in): `Mock()`, `AsyncMock()`, `patch`, `patch.dict`
   105→- `requests-mock`: For synchronous HTTP mocking (available in dev dependencies)
   106→- `aioresponses`: For async HTTP mocking (in main dependencies)
   107→- `pytest-asyncio`: For async test execution
   108→
   109→**Patterns:**
   110→
   111→```python
   112→# From test_example_unit.py - mocking class dependencies
   113→def test_get_item_quantity(self):
   114→    """Test retrieving item quantity."""
   115→    mock_db = Mock()
   116→    mock_db.query_one.return_value = {"quantity": 42}
   117→
   118→    service = InventoryService(mock_db)
   119→    result = service.get_item_quantity(item_id=1)
   120→
   121→    assert result == 42
   122→    mock_db.query_one.assert_called_once()
   123→
   124→# From test_example_unit.py - async mocking
   125→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   126→async def test_fetch_user_data_success(self):
   127→    """Test successful user data fetch."""
   128→    mock_client = AsyncMock()
   129→    mock_response = Mock()
   130→    mock_response.status_code = 200
   131→    mock_response.json.return_value = {"id": 1, "name": "John Doe"}
   132→    mock_client.get.return_value = mock_response
   133→
   134→    result = await fetch_user_data(1, mock_client)
   135→
   136→    assert result == {"id": 1, "name": "John Doe"}
   137→    mock_client.get.assert_awaited_once_with("/users/1")
   138→
   139→# From test_example_unit.py - patch decorator
   140→@patch("time.time")
   141→def test_with_patch_decorator(self, mock_time):
   142→    """Test using patch as decorator."""
   143→    mock_time.return_value = 1234567890.0
   144→
   145→    import time
   146→    result = time.time()
   147→
   148→    assert result == 1234567890.0
   149→
   150→# From test_example_unit.py - side effects
   151→def test_mock_with_side_effect(self):
   152→    """Test mocking with side effects."""
   153→    mock_func = Mock(side_effect=[1, 2, 3])
   154→
   155→    assert mock_func() == 1
   156→    assert mock_func() == 2
   157→    assert mock_func() == 3
   158→```
   159→
   160→**What to Mock:**
   161→- External HTTP calls (requests, aiohttp)
   162→- Database connections (when testing business logic)
   163→- File system operations (read/write)
   164→- Time-dependent functions
   165→- Class dependencies injected via constructor
   166→
   167→**What NOT to Mock:**
   168→- Internal helper functions (test the full flow)
   169→- Database when testing queries/ORM (use in-memory SQLite fixtures)
   170→- Environment variables should use `monkeypatch` fixture for isolation
   171→
   172→## Fixtures and Factories
   173→
   174→**Test Data:**
   175→
   176→```python
   177→# From conftest.py - simple fixtures
   178→@pytest.fixture
   179→def sample_user_data():
   180→    """Provide sample user data for testing."""
   181→    return {
   182→        "id": 1,
   183→        "username": "testuser",
   184→        "email": "test@example.com",
   185→        "is_active": True,
   186→    }
   187→
   188→# From conftest.py - parametrized fixture
   189→@pytest.fixture(params=["vertex", "local"])
   190→def model_backend(request):
   191→    """Parametrized fixture to test with different model backends.
   192→
   193→    Tests using this fixture will run once for each parameter.
   194→    """
   195→    return request.param
   196→```
   197→
   198→**Location:**
   199→- Shared fixtures: `tests/conftest.py` (automatically discovered by pytest)
   200→- Test-specific fixtures: In test module or conftest.py with clear scope
   201→- Scope control: `scope="function"` (default), `scope="session"`, `scope="module"`
   202→
   203→**Database Fixtures:**
   204→
   205→```python
   206→# From conftest.py - in-memory database
   207→@pytest.fixture(scope="function")
   208→def db_engine():
   209→    """Create an in-memory SQLite database engine for testing."""
   210→    engine = create_engine(
   211→        "sqlite:///:memory:",
   212→        connect_args={"check_same_thread": False},
   213→        poolclass=StaticPool,
   214→    )
   215→    try:
   216→        yield engine
   217→    finally:
   218→        engine.dispose()
   219→
   220→@pytest.fixture(scope="function")
   221→def db_session(db_engine) -> Generator[Session, None, None]:
   222→    """Create a database session for testing.
   223→
   224→    Automatically rolls back changes after each test.
   225→    """
   226→    session_local = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=db_engine)
   227→    session = session_local()
   228→
   229→    try:
   230→        yield session
   231→    finally:
   232→        session.rollback()
   233→        session.close()
   234→```

   236→## Coverage
   237→
   238→**Requirements:**
   239→- No strict coverage targets enforced (optional via `pytest --cov`)
   240→- Coverage collection configured in `pyproject.toml` lines 172-194
   241→
   242→**View Coverage:**
   243→```bash
   244→uv run pytest --cov
   245→```
   246→
   247→**Coverage Settings:**
   248→- Source paths: All service and lib `src/` directories
   249→- Omit: Test directories, venv, virtual environments
   250→- Excluded lines: pragma comments, `__repr__`, assertions, `NotImplementedError`, `__main__`, type checking blocks
   251→
   252→## Test Types
   253→
   254→**Unit Tests:**
   255→- Scope: Single function or class method
   256→- Dependencies: Mocked or injected
   257→- Location: `test_example_unit.py`
   258→- Example: `TestCalculateTotalPrice`, `TestInventoryService`
   259→- Pattern: Fast, isolated, no external calls
   260→
   261→**Integration Tests:**
   262→- Scope: Multiple components working together
   263→- Examples: API endpoints with database, service workflows
   264→- Location: `test_example_integration.py`
   265→- Marked with: `@pytest.mark.integration`
   266→- Pattern: Use fixtures like `test_client`, `db_session`
   267→- Database: Real schema validated but rolled back after test
   268→
   269→```python
   270→# From test_example_integration.py
   271→@pytest.mark.integration
   272→class TestHealthEndpoint:
   273→    """Test suite for health check endpoint."""
   274→
   275→    def test_health_check_returns_200(self, test_client: TestClient):
   276→        """Test that health endpoint returns 200 OK."""
   277→        response = test_client.get("/health")
   278→
   279→        assert response.status_code == 200
   280→        assert response.json() == {"status": "healthy"}
   281→```
   282→
   283→**E2E Tests:**
   284→- Scope: Full workflow from API request to database
   285→- Marked with: `@pytest.mark.e2e`
   286→- Pattern: Complete user journeys (create, read, update, delete)
   287→- Location: `test_example_integration.py` (mixed with integration tests)
   288→
   289→```python
   290→# From test_example_integration.py
   291→@pytest.mark.e2e
   292→class TestEndToEndWorkflows:
   293→    """Test suite for end-to-end workflows."""
   294→
   295→    def test_complete_user_workflow(self, test_client: TestClient):
   296→        """Test complete user workflow: create -> login -> update -> get -> delete."""
   297→        new_user = {
   298→            "username": "e2euser",
   299→            "email": "e2e@example.com",
   300→            "password": "password123",
   301→        }
   302→        create_response = test_client.post("/api/users", json=new_user)
   303→        # ... continue workflow
   304→```
   305→
   306→## Common Patterns
   307→
   308→**Async Testing:**
   309→```python
   310→# From test_example_unit.py
   311→@pytest.mark.asyncio
   312→async def test_fetch_user_data_success(self):
   313→    """Test successful user data fetch."""
   314→    mock_client = AsyncMock()
   315→    mock_response = Mock()
   316→    mock_response.status_code = 200
   317→    mock_response.json.return_value = {"id": 1, "name": "John Doe"}
   318→    mock_client.get.return_value = mock_response
   319→
   320→    result = await fetch_user_data(1, mock_client)
   321→
   322→    assert result == {"id": 1, "name": "John Doe"}
   323→```
   324→
   325→**Error Testing:**
   326→```python
   327→# From test_example_unit.py
   328→def test_empty_items_raises_error(self):
   329→    """Test that empty items list raises ValueError."""
   330→    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Items list cannot be empty"):
   331→        calculate_total_price([])
   332→
   333→# From test_example_unit.py
   334→def test_mock_raises_exception(self):
   335→    """Test mocking function that raises exception."""
   336→    mock_func = Mock(side_effect=ValueError("Test error"))
   337→
   338→    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Test error"):
   339→        mock_func()
   340→```
   341→
   342→**FastAPI Test Clients:**
   343→```python
   344→# From conftest.py - synchronous client
   345→@pytest.fixture(scope="function")
   346→def test_client(db_session) -> Generator[TestClient, None, None]:
   347→    """Create a FastAPI test client with a test database session."""
   348→    from api_service.dependencies import get_db
   349→    from api_service.main import app
   350→
   351→    def override_get_db():
   352→        try:
   353→            yield db_session
   354→        finally:
   355→            pass
   356→
   357→    app.dependency_overrides[get_db] = override_get_db
   358→
   359→    with TestClient(app) as client:
   360→        yield client
   361→
   362→    app.dependency_overrides.clear()
   363→
   364→# From conftest.py - async client
   365→@pytest.fixture(scope="function")
   366→async def async_client(db_session) -> AsyncGenerator[AsyncClient, None]:
   367→    """Create an async HTTP client for testing async endpoints."""
   368→    from api_service.dependencies import get_db
   369→    from api_service.main import app
   370→
   371→    def override_get_db():
   372→        try:
   373→            yield db_session
   374→        finally:
   375→            pass
   376→
   377→    app.dependency_overrides[get_db] = override_get_db
   378→
   379→    async with AsyncClient(app=app, base_url="http://test") as client:
   380→        yield client
   381→
   382→    app.dependency_overrides.clear()
   383→```
   384→
   385→**Parametrized Tests:**
   386→```python
   387→# From test_example_unit.py
   388→@pytest.mark.parametrize(
   389→    "items,tax_rate,expected",
   390→    [
   391→        ([{"price": 10, "quantity": 1}], 0.1, 11.0),
   392→        ([{"price": 20, "quantity": 2}], 0.15, 46.0),
   393→        ([{"price": 100, "quantity": 1}], 0.0, 100.0),
   394→    ],
   395→)
   396→def test_parametrized_calculations(self, items, tax_rate, expected):
   397→    """Test multiple scenarios using parametrize."""
   398→    result = calculate_total_price(items, tax_rate)
   399→    assert result == expected
   400→```
   401→
   402→**Singleton Reset Pattern:**
   403→```python
   404→# From conftest.py - autouse fixture for test isolation
   405→@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
   406→def reset_singletons():
   407→    """Reset singleton instances between tests.
   408→
   409→    autouse=True means this runs automatically for every test.
   410→    """
   411→    yield
   412→    # Cleanup after test - reset lazy_singleton cached instances
   413→    try:
   414→        from shared.lazy_cache import _clear_all_singletons
   415→        _clear_all_singletons()
   416→    except ImportError:
   417→        # shared module not available in this test context
   418→        pass
   419→```

    88→
    89→## Decisions Made
    90→
    91→- **Engine from sqlalchemy.engine:** SQLModel does not re-export `Engine`; must import from `sqlalchemy.engine` directly. Plan listed `from sqlmodel import Engine` which fails — corrected to `from sqlalchemy.engine import Engine`.
    92→- **expire_on_commit=False + session.expunge():** All read methods use this pattern to prevent `DetachedInstanceError` when callers access attributes after session close. The `store()` method also expunges the caller's record object to prevent the same issue.
    93→- **# noqa: E712:** Added to `is_archived == False` comparison per plan instructions to suppress ruff E712 (comparison to False).

    99→**1. [Rule 1 - Bug] Fixed incorrect Engine import from sqlmodel**
   100→- **Found during:** Task 1 (implementation)

   102→- **Fix:** Changed to `from sqlalchemy.engine import Engine` in both registry.py and test_registry.py
   103→- **Files modified:** `libs/adapter-registry/src/adapter_registry/registry.py`, `libs/adapter-registry/tests/test_registry.py`
   104→- **Verification:** Import succeeds, mypy passes
   105→- **Committed in:** a900149 (Task 1 commit)
   106→
   107→**2. [Rule 1 - Bug] Fixed DetachedInstanceError on returned SQLModel records**
   108→- **Found during:** Task 1 (test execution)
   109→- **Issue:** `retrieve_by_id()`, `query_by_task_type()`, and `list_all()` returned ORM objects that became detached after session close, causing `DetachedInstanceError` when callers accessed any attribute. `store()` also left the caller's record object in an expired/detached state.
   110→- **Fix:** Added `expire_on_commit=False` to all Session constructors and explicit `session.expunge()` calls before returning records. This detaches objects cleanly with all attributes loaded in memory.
   111→- **Files modified:** `libs/adapter-registry/src/adapter_registry/registry.py`
   112→- **Verification:** All 11 tests pass including `test_store_persists_record` and `test_retrieve_by_id_returns_matching_record` which access attributes after session close
   113→- **Committed in:** a900149 (Task 1 commit)
   114→

   118→**Impact on plan:** Both fixes required for correctness. Engine import is a factual error in plan; session expunge is a necessary SQLAlchemy pattern when using per-method sessions. No scope creep.

     3→Provides functions for managing LoRA adapters on a running vLLM server
     4→and generating code completions via the OpenAI-compatible API.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→from inference.adapter_loader import (
     8→    get_vllm_client,
     9→    list_loaded_adapters,
    10→    load_adapter,
    11→    unload_adapter,
    12→)
    13→from inference.completion import (
    14→    batch_generate,
    15→    generate_completion,
    16→    generate_with_adapter,
    17→)
    18→
    19→__all__ = [
    20→    "batch_generate",
    21→    "generate_completion",
    22→    "generate_with_adapter",
    23→    "get_vllm_client",
    24→    "list_loaded_adapters",
    25→    "load_adapter",
    26→    "unload_adapter",
    27→]
    28→

1→"""Adapter loading and vLLM client for OpenAI-compatible inference.
     2→
     3→Uses the openai package (not a direct vllm import) to communicate with
     4→vLLM's OpenAI-compatible API endpoint. This allows the module to be
     5→imported and instantiated on CPU-only machines without vLLM installed.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import os
     9→from typing import Any
    10→
    11→from openai import AsyncOpenAI  # type: ignore[import-not-found]
    12→
    13→VLLM_BASE_URL = os.getenv("VLLM_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:8000/v1")
    14→
    15→
    16→def get_vllm_client(base_url: str | None = None) -> AsyncOpenAI:
    17→    """Create an AsyncOpenAI client pointing at a vLLM server.
    18→
    19→    Uses the openai package with a custom base_url to connect to vLLM's
    20→    OpenAI-compatible API. This avoids a direct vllm import, allowing
    21→    instantiation on CPU-only machines.
    22→
    23→    Args:
    24→        base_url: Override URL for the vLLM server. Defaults to
    25→            VLLM_BASE_URL env var or http://localhost:8000/v1.
    26→
    27→    Returns:
    28→        An AsyncOpenAI client configured for the vLLM endpoint.
    29→
    30→    Example:
    31→        >>> client = get_vllm_client()
    32→        >>> client.base_url  # Points to vLLM server
    33→    """
    34→    return AsyncOpenAI(
    35→        base_url=base_url or VLLM_BASE_URL,
    36→        api_key="not-needed-for-local-vllm",
    37→    )
    38→
    39→
    40→def load_adapter(adapter_id: str, model_name: str) -> Any:
    41→    """Load a LoRA adapter into the running vLLM server.
    42→
    43→    Calls the vLLM dynamic LoRA loading API via the OpenAI client
    44→    to hot-load an adapter without restarting the server.
    45→
    46→    Args:
    47→        adapter_id: UUID of the adapter in the registry.
    48→        model_name: Name/path of the base model the adapter targets.
    49→
    50→    Returns:
    51→        Response from the vLLM adapter loading API.
    52→
    53→    Raises:
    54→        NotImplementedError: Method is not yet implemented.
    55→
    56→    Example:
    57→        >>> response = load_adapter("adapter-001", "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B")
    58→        # Returns vLLM API response when implemented
    59→    """
    60→    raise NotImplementedError(
    61→        "load_adapter is not yet implemented. "
    62→        "It will call the vLLM dynamic LoRA loading API via the openai client."
    63→    )
    64→
    65→
    66→def unload_adapter(adapter_id: str) -> Any:
    67→    """Unload a LoRA adapter from the running vLLM server.
    68→
    69→    Calls the vLLM dynamic LoRA unloading API via the OpenAI client
    70→    to remove an adapter without restarting the server.
    71→
    72→    Args:
    73→        adapter_id: UUID of the adapter to unload.
    74→
    75→    Returns:
    76→        Response from the vLLM adapter unloading API.
    77→
    78→    Raises:
    79→        NotImplementedError: Method is not yet implemented.
    80→
    81→    Example:
    82→        >>> response = unload_adapter("adapter-001")
    83→        # Returns vLLM API response when implemented
    84→    """
    85→    raise NotImplementedError(
    86→        "unload_adapter is not yet implemented. "
    87→        "It will call the vLLM dynamic LoRA unloading API via the openai client."
    88→    )
    89→
    90→
    91→def list_loaded_adapters() -> list[str]:
    92→    """List all currently loaded LoRA adapters on the vLLM server.
    93→
    94→    Queries the vLLM server for the set of LoRA adapters currently
    95→    hot-loaded and available for inference.
    96→
    97→    Returns:
    98→        List of adapter IDs currently loaded on the vLLM server.
    99→
   100→    Raises:
   101→        NotImplementedError: Method is not yet implemented.
   102→
   103→    Example:
   104→        >>> adapters = list_loaded_adapters()
   105→        # Returns ["adapter-001", "adapter-002"] when implemented
   106→    """
   107→    raise NotImplementedError(
   108→        "list_loaded_adapters is not yet implemented. "
   109→        "It will query the vLLM server for currently loaded LoRA adapters."
   110→    )
   111→

1→"""Completion functions for generating code via the vLLM server."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→
     6→def generate_completion(
     7→    prompt: str,
     8→    model: str = "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B",
     9→    max_tokens: int = 1024,

    12→
    13→    Sends the prompt to the vLLM server using the base model and returns
    14→    the generated text completion.
    15→
    16→    Args:
    17→        prompt: The input prompt string to complete.
    18→        model: Base model name/path on the vLLM server.
    19→            Defaults to Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B.
    20→        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate. Defaults to 1024.
    21→
    22→    Returns:
    23→        Generated text completion string from the base model.
    24→
    25→    Raises:
    26→        NotImplementedError: Method is not yet implemented.
    27→
    28→    Example:
    29→        >>> result = generate_completion("def hello():")
    30→        # Returns generated code string when implemented
    31→    """
    32→    raise NotImplementedError(
    33→        "generate_completion is not yet implemented. "
    34→        "It will call the vLLM server to generate a completion."
    35→    )
    36→
    37→
    38→def generate_with_adapter(
    39→    prompt: str,
    40→    adapter_id: str,
    41→    model: str = "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B",
    42→    max_tokens: int = 1024,
    43→) -> str:
    44→    """Generate a completion using a specific LoRA adapter.
    45→
    46→    Sends the prompt to the vLLM server with a loaded LoRA adapter applied,
    47→    enabling fine-tuned code generation for specific tasks.
    48→
    49→    Args:
    50→        prompt: The input prompt string to complete.
    51→        adapter_id: UUID of the loaded LoRA adapter to use.
    52→        model: Base model name/path on the vLLM server.
    53→            Defaults to Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B.
    54→        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate. Defaults to 1024.
    55→
    56→    Returns:
    57→        Generated text completion string using the specified adapter.
    58→
    59→    Raises:
    60→        NotImplementedError: Method is not yet implemented.
    61→
    62→    Example:
    63→        >>> result = generate_with_adapter("def hello():", adapter_id="adapter-001")
    64→        # Returns adapter-generated code string when implemented
    65→    """
    66→    raise NotImplementedError(
    67→        "generate_with_adapter is not yet implemented. "
    68→        "It will call the vLLM server with a LoRA adapter."
    69→    )
    70→
    71→
    72→def batch_generate(
    73→    prompts: list[str],
    74→    model: str = "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B",
    75→    adapter_id: str | None = None,
    76→    max_tokens: int = 1024,
    77→) -> list[str]:
    78→    """Generate completions for multiple prompts in batch.
    79→
    80→    Sends multiple prompts to the vLLM server in a single batch request,
    81→    optionally using a LoRA adapter. Returns one completion per prompt.
    82→
    83→    Args:
    84→        prompts: List of prompt strings to complete.
    85→        model: Base model name/path on the vLLM server.
    86→            Defaults to Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B.
    87→        adapter_id: Optional UUID of a loaded LoRA adapter to use.
    88→            If None, uses the base model without any adapter.
    89→        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per prompt.
    90→            Defaults to 1024.
    91→
    92→    Returns:
    93→        List of generated text completions, one per input prompt.
    94→
    95→    Raises:
    96→        NotImplementedError: Method is not yet implemented.
    97→
    98→    Example:
    99→        >>> results = batch_generate(["def foo():", "def bar():"])
   100→        # Returns ["generated foo...", "generated bar..."] when implemented
   101→    """
   102→    raise NotImplementedError(
   103→        "batch_generate is not yet implemented. "
   104→        "It will send multiple prompts to the vLLM server in a single batch request."
   105→    )

1→"""VLLMClient stub wrapping OpenAI's AsyncOpenAI for vLLM inference.
     2→
     3→Uses the OpenAI-compatible API exposed by vLLM rather than importing
     4→vLLM directly, keeping the client lightweight and decoupled from GPU deps.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→from __future__ import annotations
     8→
     9→import os
    10→from typing import Any
    11→
    12→from openai import AsyncOpenAI  # type: ignore[import-not-found]
    13→
    14→VLLM_BASE_URL = os.getenv("VLLM_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:8000/v1")
    15→
    16→
    17→class VLLMClient:
    18→    """Async client for vLLM's OpenAI-compatible API.
    19→
    20→    Wraps AsyncOpenAI to communicate with the local vLLM server.
    21→    Methods are stubs that will be implemented when the vLLM dynamic
    22→    LoRA loading API is integrated.
    23→
    24→    Attributes:
    25→        _client: Internal AsyncOpenAI client instance.
    26→
    27→    Example:
    28→        >>> client = VLLMClient()
    29→        >>> client._client.base_url
    30→    """
    31→
    32→    def __init__(self, base_url: str | None = None) -> None:
    33→        """Initialize the VLLMClient.
    34→
    35→        Args:
    36→            base_url: Override URL for the vLLM server.
    37→                Defaults to VLLM_BASE_URL env var.
    38→        """
    39→        self._client = AsyncOpenAI(
    40→            base_url=base_url or VLLM_BASE_URL,
    41→            api_key="not-needed-for-local-vllm",
    42→        )
    43→
    44→    async def load_adapter(self, adapter_id: str, model_name: str) -> Any:
    45→        """Load a LoRA adapter into the running vLLM server.
    46→
    47→        Will use vLLM's dynamic LoRA loading API to hot-swap adapters
    48→        without restarting the server.
    49→
    50→        Args:
    51→            adapter_id: Identifier for the LoRA adapter to load.
    52→            model_name: Base model the adapter was trained on.
    53→
    54→        Returns:
    55→            Response dict from the vLLM adapter loading API.
    56→
    57→        Raises:
    58→            NotImplementedError: Pending vLLM dynamic LoRA loading API integration.
    59→
    60→        Example:
    61→            >>> client = VLLMClient()
    62→            >>> await client.load_adapter(
    63→            ...     "adapter-v1", "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B",
    64→            ... )
    65→        """
    66→        raise NotImplementedError(
    67→            f"load_adapter('{adapter_id}', '{model_name}') is not yet implemented. "
    68→            "Requires vLLM dynamic LoRA loading API integration -- "
    69→            "see vLLM docs on /v1/load_lora_adapter endpoint."
    70→        )
    71→
    72→    async def generate(
    73→        self, prompt: str, model: str, adapter_id: str | None = None
    74→    ) -> Any:
    75→        """Generate a completion using vLLM with an optional LoRA adapter.
    76→
    77→        Will call the OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint on the
    78→        local vLLM server, optionally routing through a loaded LoRA adapter.
    79→
    80→        Args:
    81→            prompt: The input prompt for generation.
    82→            model: The base model identifier.
    83→            adapter_id: Optional LoRA adapter to use.
    84→
    85→        Returns:
    86→            Generated text completion string.
    87→
    88→        Raises:
    89→            NotImplementedError: Pending chat completion call implementation.
    90→
    91→        Example:
    92→            >>> client = VLLMClient()
    93→            >>> await client.generate(
    94→            ...     "Write hello world",
    95→            ...     "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B",
    96→            ... )
    97→        """
    98→        raise NotImplementedError(
    99→            f"generate(model='{model}', "
   100→            f"adapter_id='{adapter_id}') is not yet "
   101→            "implemented. Will use "
   102→            "self._client.chat.completions.create()."
   103→        )
   104→

1→FROM python:3.12-slim
     2→# Note: vLLM is installed on the GPU host or via a GPU-enabled base image.
     3→# This Dockerfile documents the service structure and installs the health sidecar deps.
     4→WORKDIR /app
     5→COPY . .
     6→RUN pip install --no-cache-dir fastapi uvicorn[standard] openai pyyaml
     7→RUN chmod +x startup.sh
     8→EXPOSE 8000 8001
     9→CMD ["./startup.sh"]
    10→

1→"""LoRA server configuration with safety constraints for multi-GPU inference."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→from dataclasses import dataclass, fields
     6→from pathlib import Path
     7→from typing import Any
     8→
     9→import yaml
    10→
    11→
    12→@dataclass
    13→class LoraServerConfig:
    14→    """Configuration for the vLLM-based LoRA inference server.
    15→
    16→    Enforces PP=2/TP=1 layout to avoid vLLM bug #21471 where TP+LoRA
    17→    produces corrupted outputs on consumer GPUs without NVLink.
    18→
    19→    Attributes:
    20→        model: HuggingFace model identifier.
    21→        pipeline_parallel_size: Number of pipeline parallel stages.
    22→        tensor_parallel_size: Number of tensor parallel shards.
    23→        enable_lora: Whether to enable LoRA adapter support.
    24→        quantization: Quantization method (e.g. 'awq').
    25→        port: Port for the vLLM inference server.
    26→        health_port: Port for the health sidecar.
    27→        max_loras: Maximum number of concurrent LoRA adapters.
    28→
    29→    Example:
    30→        >>> config = LoraServerConfig()
    31→        >>> config.pipeline_parallel_size
    32→        2
    33→        >>> config.tensor_parallel_size
    34→        1
    35→    """
    36→
    37→    model: str = "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct"
    38→    pipeline_parallel_size: int = 2
    39→    tensor_parallel_size: int = 1
    40→    enable_lora: bool = True
    41→    quantization: str = "awq"
    42→    port: int = 8000
    43→    health_port: int = 8001
    44→    max_loras: int = 8
    45→
    46→    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
    47→        """Validate configuration constraints after initialization."""
    48→        if self.tensor_parallel_size == 2:
    49→            raise ValueError(
    50→                "tensor_parallel_size=2 is forbidden. "
    51→                "vLLM bug #21471: TP+LoRA causes corrupted "
    52→                "outputs on consumer GPUs without NVLink. "
    53→                "Use pipeline_parallel_size=2 with "
    54→                "tensor_parallel_size=1 instead."
    55→            )
    56→
    57→    @classmethod
    58→    def from_yaml(cls, path: str) -> LoraServerConfig:
    59→        """Load configuration from a YAML file.
    60→
    61→        Only keys matching dataclass fields are passed through;
    62→        unknown keys in the YAML are silently ignored.
    63→
    64→        Args:
    65→            path: Filesystem path to the YAML config file.
    66→
    67→        Returns:
    68→            A LoraServerConfig populated from the YAML file.
    69→
    70→        Raises:
    71→            FileNotFoundError: If the YAML file does not exist.
    72→
    73→        Example:
    74→            >>> config = LoraServerConfig.from_yaml("config.yml")
    75→            >>> config.model
    76→            'Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct'
    77→        """
    78→        raw: dict[str, Any] = yaml.safe_load(Path(path).read_text())
    79→        valid_fields = {f.name for f in fields(cls)}
    80→        filtered = {k: v for k, v in raw.items() if k in valid_fields}
    81→        return cls(**filtered)
    82→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→set -euo pipefail
     3→
     4→# Read model name from config.yaml, fall back to default
     5→MODEL=$(python -c "import yaml; print(yaml.safe_load(open('config.yaml'))['model'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct")
     6→
     7→# Start health sidecar in background (port 8001)
     8→python health.py &
     9→
    10→# Start vLLM OpenAI-compatible API server
    11→exec python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
    12→    --model "$MODEL" \
    13→    --pipeline-parallel-size 2 \
    14→    --tensor-parallel-size 1 \
    15→    --enable-lora \
    16→    --quantization awq \
    17→    --max-loras 8 \
    18→    --port 8000
    19→

1→# Deployment stack: API, UI, Postgres, MLflow, Pub/Sub emulator.
     2→# Run from repo root: docker-compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml up --build
     3→services:
     4→  api:
     5→    build:
     6→      context: ..
     7→      dockerfile: services/api-service/Dockerfile
     8→    ports:
     9→      - "${API_PORT:-8000}:8000"
    10→    environment:
    11→      - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-postgres}@db:5432/${POSTGRES_DB:-app}
    12→      - MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=http://mlflow:5000
    13→    depends_on:
    14→      db:
    15→        condition: service_healthy
    16→      mlflow:
    17→        condition: service_started
    18→    healthcheck:
    19→      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8000/health"]
    20→      interval: 30s
    21→      timeout: 10s
    22→      retries: 3
    23→
    24→  db:
    25→    image: postgres:16-alpine
    26→    environment:
    27→      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}
    28→      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-postgres}
    29→      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB:-app}
    30→    volumes:
    31→      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    32→    healthcheck:
    33→      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}"]
    34→      interval: 5s
    35→      timeout: 5s
    36→      retries: 5
    37→
    38→  mlflow:
    39→    image: ghcr.io/mlflow/mlflow:v2.16.2
    40→    ports:
    41→      - "5000:5000"
    42→    command: mlflow server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5000 --backend-store-uri /mlflow
    43→    volumes:
    44→      - mlflow_data:/mlflow
    45→    healthcheck:
    46→      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:5000/health"]
    47→      interval: 10s
    48→      timeout: 5s
    49→      retries: 3
    50→
    51→  pubsub-emulator:
    52→    image: 3apag/pubsub-emulator:latest
    53→    ports:
    54→      - "${PUBSUB_EMULATOR_PORT:-8432}:8432"
    55→    environment:
    56→      PUBSUB_PROJECT_ID: ${PUBSUB_PROJECT_ID:-default}
    57→      PUBSUB_LISTEN_ADDRESS: "0.0.0.0:8432"
    58→    volumes:
    59→      - pubsub_data:/opt/data
    60→    healthcheck:
    61→      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "nc -z localhost 8432 || exit 1"]
    62→      interval: 5s
    63→      timeout: 5s
    64→      retries: 5
    65→
    66→  lora-server:
    67→    build:
    68→      context: ..
    69→      dockerfile: services/lora-server/Dockerfile
    70→    ports:
    71→      - "${LORA_SERVER_PORT:-8000}:8000"
    72→      - "${LORA_HEALTH_PORT:-8001}:8001"
    73→    volumes:
    74→      - adapters_data:/adapters
    75→    deploy:
    76→      resources:
    77→        reservations:
    78→          devices:
    79→            - driver: nvidia
    80→              count: all
    81→              capabilities: [gpu]
    82→    environment:
    83→      - VLLM_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8000/v1
    84→    healthcheck:
    85→      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8001/health"]
    86→      interval: 30s
    87→      timeout: 10s
    88→      retries: 3
    89→      start_period: 120s
    90→
    91→volumes:
    92→  postgres_data:
    93→  mlflow_data:
    94→  pubsub_data:
    95→  adapters_data:
    96→

1→[project]
     2→name = "inference"
     3→version = "0.1.0"
     4→description = "Shared inference library for model loading and agent factories"
     5→readme = "README.md"
     6→requires-python = ">=3.12"
     7→dependencies = [
     8→    "shared",
     9→    "openai>=1.0.0",
    10→    "pydantic>=2.0.0",
    11→]
    12→
    13→[build-system]
    14→requires = ["hatchling"]
    15→build-backend = "hatchling.build"
    16→
    17→[dependency-groups]
    18→dev = [
    19→    "pytest>=8.0.0",
    20→    "pytest-cov>=5.0.0",
    21→]
    22→
    23→[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
    24→packages = ["src/inference"]
    25→
    26→[tool.mypy]
    27→strict = true
    28→exclude = ["venv", ".venv", "docs"]
    29→plugins = []
    30→mypy_path = ["../shared/src"]
    31→
    32→[tool.pytest.ini_options]
    33→pythonpath = ["src", "../shared/src"]
    34→testpaths = ["tests"]
    35→addopts = "--cov=inference --cov-branch --cov-report=term-missing"
    36→
    37→[tool.uv.sources]
    38→shared = { workspace = true }
    39→

1→"""TDD tests for lora-server vllm_client.py VLLMClient methods.
     2→
     3→Tests assert NotImplementedError is raised for stub methods.
     4→Imports directly from Python source via conftest.py sys.path setup.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import pytest
     8→from vllm_client import VLLMClient
     9→
    10→
    11→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    12→async def test_load_adapter_raises_not_implemented():
    13→    """Test VLLMClient.load_adapter raises NotImplementedError."""
    14→    client = VLLMClient()
    15→    with pytest.raises(NotImplementedError, match="load_adapter"):
    16→        await client.load_adapter("adapter-1", "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct")
    17→
    18→
    19→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    20→async def test_generate_raises_not_implemented():
    21→    """Test VLLMClient.generate raises NotImplementedError."""
    22→    client = VLLMClient()
    23→    with pytest.raises(NotImplementedError, match="generate"):
    24→        await client.generate("Write hello world", "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct")
    25→

1→"""Pytest configuration for services/lora-server.
     2→
     3→lora-server is a Dockerfile-only service (not a uv workspace member).
     4→We add its src directory to sys.path so tests can import directly.

from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

@dataclass
class GenerationResult:
    text: str
    model: str
    adapter_id: str | None
    token_count: int
    finish_reason: str

class InferenceProvider(ABC):
    @abstractmethod

    @abstractmethod
    async def load_adapter(self, adapter_id: str, adapter_path: str) -> None: ...
    @abstractmethod
    async def unload_adapter(self, adapter_id: str) -> None: ...
    @abstractmethod
    async def list_adapters(self) -> list[str]: ...

    98→**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 19

   114→  2. User can call apply_lora_adapter() to attach a LoRA adapter to a base model for inference or further training
   115→  3. POST /train/lora with a session_id runs the full trajectory → SFT format → PEFT train → save safetensors → registry.store() pipeline and returns a job_id
   116→  4. Trained adapter files are in standard PEFT safetensors format (no embed_tokens keys) compatible with vLLM dynamic loading via InferenceProvider.load_adapter()

   127→  3. User can call run_humaneval_subset() and receive pass/fail results for each task in the 20-task subset
   128→  4. User can call calculate_pass_at_k() and receive a Pass@1 score for both baseline and adapter-enhanced configurations
   129→  5. Kill-switch gate comparison prints a clear verdict: PASS (>=5% improvement) or FAIL (hypothesis rejected), with both baseline and adapter Pass@1 scores shown

### v5.0 First Implementation (In Progress)

**Milestone Goal:** Transform wireframe stubs into a working end-to-end system — adapter registry to provider-agnostic inference to agent loop to QLoRA training to Doc-to-LoRA kill-switch gate — running entirely on local hardware.

- [x] **Phase 18: Adapter Registry** — SQLite-backed CRUD hub that all other components read from and write to (2/2 plans complete)
- [ ] **Phase 19: Inference Provider Abstraction** — Abstract InferenceProvider interface with vLLM and Ollama implementations, provider factory, and lora-server configuration
- [ ] **Phase 20: Agent Loop** — Backend-agnostic generate → execute → reflect → save_trajectory cycle with trajectory persistence
- [ ] **Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline** — Full gradient-descent training path from trajectory to PEFT adapter stored in registry, with training-svc HTTP dispatch
- [ ] **Phase 22: Kill-Switch Gate** — Doc-to-LoRA hypernetwork + evaluation lib measuring the 5% Pass@1 improvement threshold

## Phase Details

### Phase 18: Adapter Registry
**Goal**: Users can persist, retrieve, and query LoRA adapter metadata via a SQLite-backed registry that all other components share
**Depends on**: Nothing (first v5.0 phase)
**Requirements**: AREG-01, AREG-02, AREG-03, AREG-04, AREG-05
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. User can call AdapterRegistry.store() with full metadata and retrieve the same record by ID with no data loss
  2. User can call AdapterRegistry.query_by_task_type() and receive only adapters matching the specified task type
  3. User can call AdapterRegistry.list_all() and receive all non-archived records (archived records excluded)
  4. Multiple FastAPI requests can read and write concurrently without deadlock (WAL mode confirmed via PRAGMA)
  5. AdapterRegistry constructor accepts an explicit engine parameter so all services share one SQLite file via DATABASE_URL
**Plans**: 2 plans

Plans:
- [x] 18-01-PLAN.md — Implement AdapterRegistry: constructor (Engine + WAL hook + create_all) and 4 CRUD methods — DONE 2026-03-05
- [x] 18-02-PLAN.md — Pivot tests from red to green: update conftest fixtures, replace NotImplementedError assertions, add WAL and concurrency integration tests — DONE 2026-03-05

### Phase 19: Inference Provider Abstraction
**Goal**: Users can generate completions and hot-load/unload LoRA adapters through a provider-agnostic interface, with vLLM and Ollama backends selectable by configuration and a correctly configured lora-server
**Depends on**: Phase 18
**Requirements**: INF-01, INF-02, INF-03, INF-04, INF-05, INF-06, INF-07, INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. InferenceProvider interface exists with generate(), load_adapter(), unload_adapter(), and list_adapters() methods that both backends implement
  2. Provider factory returns a VLLMProvider or OllamaProvider instance based on an environment variable or config file with no code changes required
  3. User can call provider.generate() with an adapter name and receive adapter-conditioned output from the active backend
  4. User can load two adapters simultaneously via VLLMProvider and generate from each by name without collision
  5. Agent can configure different models or providers per step (e.g., VLLMProvider for generation, OllamaProvider for reflection) without changing agent graph structure
  6. lora-server starts without error with VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True set, and docker-compose runs all services without port conflict
**Plans**: 3 plans

Plans:
- [ ] 19-01-PLAN.md — InferenceProvider ABC + GenerationResult + VLLMProvider + OllamaProvider with full test coverage
- [ ] 19-02-PLAN.md — lora-server Dockerfile update (vLLM base image + runtime LoRA env) and docker-compose port conflict fix
- [ ] 19-03-PLAN.md — Provider factory with instance cache + per-step config + __init__.py exports + delete old stubs

### Phase 20: Agent Loop
**Goal**: Users can invoke the Rune agent on a coding task and observe a complete generate → execute → reflect → save_trajectory cycle through the InferenceProvider interface, with trajectory data persisted to disk
**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 19
**Requirements**: AGENT-01, AGENT-02, AGENT-03, AGENT-04, AGENT-05, AGENT-06, TRAIN-01, TRAIN-02
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. Agent invocation on a coding task produces generated code from generate_node via InferenceProvider.generate() (backend-agnostic — same call path for vLLM or Ollama)
  2. execute_node runs the generated code in a subprocess sandbox and returns stdout, stderr, exit_code, and tests_passed within the timeout
  3. reflect_node accumulates attempt count and trajectory data without making any LLM call
  4. save_trajectory_node writes a structured JSON trajectory file (session_id, steps, outcome) readable after the session ends
  5. Agent loop closes end-to-end: retries on failure up to max_attempts, then terminates with either success or exhausted outcome; RuneState includes session_id field
**Plans**: TBD

### Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline
**Goal**: Users can trigger end-to-end QLoRA training from a recorded trajectory, producing a PEFT safetensors adapter stored in the registry and accessible for loading via the inference provider
**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 20
**Requirements**: TRAIN-03, TRAIN-04, TRAIN-05, TRAIN-06, TRAIN-07, INFRA-04, INFRA-05
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. User can call build_qlora_config() and receive a PEFT LoraConfig with NF4 quantization and bfloat16 compute dtype
  2. User can call apply_lora_adapter() to attach a LoRA adapter to a base model for inference or further training
  3. POST /train/lora with a session_id runs the full trajectory → SFT format → PEFT train → save safetensors → registry.store() pipeline and returns a job_id
  4. Trained adapter files are in standard PEFT safetensors format (no embed_tokens keys) compatible with vLLM dynamic loading via InferenceProvider.load_adapter()
  5. GPU imports (peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl, datasets) are deferred inside function bodies and do not cause ImportError in CPU-only CI
**Plans**: TBD

### Phase 22: Kill-Switch Gate
**Goal**: Users can measure whether Doc-to-LoRA-generated adapters improve Pass@1 on a HumanEval subset by at least 5% relative to the base model, validating the core hypothesis
**Depends on**: Phase 20, Phase 21
**Requirements**: DTOL-01, DTOL-02, DTOL-03, DTOL-04, EVAL-01, EVAL-02, EVAL-03
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. DocToLoraHypernetwork exists in model-training lib, accepts a coding trajectory, and returns rank-8 LoRA adapter weights in a single forward pass under 1 second
  2. Generated adapter weights are in standard PEFT safetensors format and can be loaded into vLLM via InferenceProvider.load_adapter() without error
  3. User can call run_humaneval_subset() and receive pass/fail results for each task in the 20-task subset
  4. User can call calculate_pass_at_k() and receive a Pass@1 score for both baseline and adapter-enhanced configurations
  5. Kill-switch gate comparison prints a clear verdict: PASS (>=5% improvement) or FAIL (hypothesis rejected), with both baseline and adapter Pass@1 scores shown
**Plans**: TBD

## PLANNING COMPLETE

**Phase:** 19-inference-provider-abstraction
**Plans:** 3 plans in 2 waves

### Wave Structure

| Wave | Plans | Autonomous |
|------|-------|------------|
| 1 | 19-01, 19-02 | yes, yes |
| 2 | 19-03 | yes |

### Plans Created

| Plan | Objective | Tasks | Wave | Requirements |
|------|-----------|-------|------|-------------|
| 19-01 | InferenceProvider ABC + VLLMProvider + OllamaProvider + tests | 3 | 1 | INF-01, INF-02, INF-03, INF-05, INF-06 |
| 19-02 | lora-server Dockerfile (vLLM base image) + docker-compose port fix | 2 | 1 | INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03 |
| 19-03 | Provider factory + exports wiring + delete old stubs + update tests | 2 | 2 | INF-04, INF-07 |

### Dependency Graph

```
19-01 (providers) ──┐
                    ├──> 19-03 (factory + wiring)
19-02 (infra) ──────┘ (independent, runs parallel with 01)
```

Plans 01 and 02 run in parallel (Wave 1) with zero file overlap:
- Plan 01 creates new files in `libs/inference/src/inference/` and `libs/inference/tests/`
- Plan 02 modifies `services/lora-server/Dockerfile`, `services/lora-server/config.py`, `infra/docker-compose.yml`

Plan 03 (Wave 2) depends on Plan 01's provider implementations to build the factory and update `__init__.py` exports.

### Requirement Coverage

All 10 phase requirements mapped:
- **INF-01** through **INF-07**: Plans 01 + 03
- **INFRA-01** through **INFRA-03**: Plan 02

### Key Design Decisions

- **Clean break** per CONTEXT.md: old stubs (adapter_loader.py, completion.py, vllm_client.py) deleted in Plan 03, replaced with provider-based API
- **Port 8100** for lora-server host (container stays 8000) per locked decision
- **Internal tracking set** in VLLMProvider for `list_adapters()` reliability (vLLM bug #11761 workaround)
- **Factory cache** by `(provider_type, base_url)` tuple with autouse conftest fixture to prevent test leaks

    55→### v5.0 First Implementation (In Progress)
    56→
    57→**Milestone Goal:** Transform wireframe stubs into a working end-to-end system — adapter registry to provider-agnostic inference to agent loop to QLoRA training to Doc-to-LoRA kill-switch gate — running entirely on local hardware.
    58→
    59→- [x] **Phase 18: Adapter Registry** — SQLite-backed CRUD hub that all other components read from and write to (2/2 plans complete)
    60→- [ ] **Phase 19: Inference Provider Abstraction** — Abstract InferenceProvider interface with vLLM and Ollama implementations, provider factory, and lora-server configuration
    61→- [ ] **Phase 20: Agent Loop** — Backend-agnostic generate → execute → reflect → save_trajectory cycle with trajectory persistence
    62→- [ ] **Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline** — Full gradient-descent training path from trajectory to PEFT adapter stored in registry, with training-svc HTTP dispatch
    63→- [ ] **Phase 22: Kill-Switch Gate** — Doc-to-LoRA hypernetwork + evaluation lib measuring the 5% Pass@1 improvement threshold
    64→
    65→## Phase Details
    66→
    67→### Phase 18: Adapter Registry
    68→**Goal**: Users can persist, retrieve, and query LoRA adapter metadata via a SQLite-backed registry that all other components share
    69→**Depends on**: Nothing (first v5.0 phase)
    70→**Requirements**: AREG-01, AREG-02, AREG-03, AREG-04, AREG-05
    71→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    72→  1. User can call AdapterRegistry.store() with full metadata and retrieve the same record by ID with no data loss
    73→  2. User can call AdapterRegistry.query_by_task_type() and receive only adapters matching the specified task type
    74→  3. User can call AdapterRegistry.list_all() and receive all non-archived records (archived records excluded)
    75→  4. Multiple FastAPI requests can read and write concurrently without deadlock (WAL mode confirmed via PRAGMA)
    76→  5. AdapterRegistry constructor accepts an explicit engine parameter so all services share one SQLite file via DATABASE_URL
    77→**Plans**: 2 plans
    78→
    79→Plans:
    80→- [x] 18-01-PLAN.md — Implement AdapterRegistry: constructor (Engine + WAL hook + create_all) and 4 CRUD methods — DONE 2026-03-05
    81→- [x] 18-02-PLAN.md — Pivot tests from red to green: update conftest fixtures, replace NotImplementedError assertions, add WAL and concurrency integration tests — DONE 2026-03-05
    82→
    83→### Phase 19: Inference Provider Abstraction
    84→**Goal**: Users can generate completions and hot-load/unload LoRA adapters through a provider-agnostic interface, with vLLM and Ollama backends selectable by configuration and a correctly configured lora-server
    85→**Depends on**: Phase 18
    86→**Requirements**: INF-01, INF-02, INF-03, INF-04, INF-05, INF-06, INF-07, INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03
    87→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    88→  1. InferenceProvider interface exists with generate(), load_adapter(), unload_adapter(), and list_adapters() methods that both backends implement
    89→  2. Provider factory returns a VLLMProvider or OllamaProvider instance based on an environment variable or config file with no code changes required
    90→  3. User can call provider.generate() with an adapter name and receive adapter-conditioned output from the active backend
    91→  4. User can load two adapters simultaneously via VLLMProvider and generate from each by name without collision
    92→  5. Agent can configure different models or providers per step (e.g., VLLMProvider for generation, OllamaProvider for reflection) without changing agent graph structure
    93→  6. lora-server starts without error with VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True set, and docker-compose runs all services without port conflict
    94→**Plans**: 3 plans
    95→
    96→Plans:
    97→- [ ] 19-01-PLAN.md — InferenceProvider ABC + GenerationResult + VLLMProvider + OllamaProvider with full test coverage
    98→- [ ] 19-02-PLAN.md — lora-server Dockerfile update (vLLM base image + runtime LoRA env) and docker-compose port conflict fix
    99→- [ ] 19-03-PLAN.md — Provider factory with instance cache + per-step config + __init__.py exports + delete old stubs
   100→
   101→### Phase 20: Agent Loop
   102→**Goal**: Users can invoke the Rune agent on a coding task and observe a complete generate → execute → reflect → save_trajectory cycle through the InferenceProvider interface, with trajectory data persisted to disk
   103→**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 19
   104→**Requirements**: AGENT-01, AGENT-02, AGENT-03, AGENT-04, AGENT-05, AGENT-06, TRAIN-01, TRAIN-02
   105→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   106→  1. Agent invocation on a coding task produces generated code from generate_node via InferenceProvider.generate() (backend-agnostic — same call path for vLLM or Ollama)
   107→  2. execute_node runs the generated code in a subprocess sandbox and returns stdout, stderr, exit_code, and tests_passed within the timeout
   108→  3. reflect_node accumulates attempt count and trajectory data without making any LLM call
   109→  4. save_trajectory_node writes a structured JSON trajectory file (session_id, steps, outcome) readable after the session ends
   110→  5. Agent loop closes end-to-end: retries on failure up to max_attempts, then terminates with either success or exhausted outcome; RuneState includes session_id field
   111→**Plans**: TBD
   112→
   113→### Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline
   114→**Goal**: Users can trigger end-to-end QLoRA training from a recorded trajectory, producing a PEFT safetensors adapter stored in the registry and accessible for loading via the inference provider
   115→**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 20
   116→**Requirements**: TRAIN-03, TRAIN-04, TRAIN-05, TRAIN-06, TRAIN-07, INFRA-04, INFRA-05
   117→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   118→  1. User can call build_qlora_config() and receive a PEFT LoraConfig with NF4 quantization and bfloat16 compute dtype
   119→  2. User can call apply_lora_adapter() to attach a LoRA adapter to a base model for inference or further training
   120→  3. POST /train/lora with a session_id runs the full trajectory → SFT format → PEFT train → save safetensors → registry.store() pipeline and returns a job_id
   121→  4. Trained adapter files are in standard PEFT safetensors format (no embed_tokens keys) compatible with vLLM dynamic loading via InferenceProvider.load_adapter()
   122→  5. GPU imports (peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl, datasets) are deferred inside function bodies and do not cause ImportError in CPU-only CI
   123→**Plans**: TBD
   124→
   125→### Phase 22: Kill-Switch Gate
   126→**Goal**: Users can measure whether Doc-to-LoRA-generated adapters improve Pass@1 on a HumanEval subset by at least 5% relative to the base model, validating the core hypothesis
   127→**Depends on**: Phase 20, Phase 21
   128→**Requirements**: DTOL-01, DTOL-02, DTOL-03, DTOL-04, EVAL-01, EVAL-02, EVAL-03
   129→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   130→  1. DocToLoraHypernetwork exists in model-training lib, accepts a coding trajectory, and returns rank-8 LoRA adapter weights in a single forward pass under 1 second
   131→  2. Generated adapter weights are in standard PEFT safetensors format and can be loaded into vLLM via InferenceProvider.load_adapter() without error
   132→  3. User can call run_humaneval_subset() and receive pass/fail results for each task in the 20-task subset
   133→  4. User can call calculate_pass_at_k() and receive a Pass@1 score for both baseline and adapter-enhanced configurations
   134→  5. Kill-switch gate comparison prints a clear verdict: PASS (>=5% improvement) or FAIL (hypothesis rejected), with both baseline and adapter Pass@1 scores shown
   135→**Plans**: TBD

1→# Requirements: Rune
     2→
     3→**Defined:** 2026-03-05
     4→**Core Value:** A local coding agent that learns from its own coding trajectories, building persistent parametric memory that scales independently of context window size.
     5→
     6→## v5.0 Requirements
     7→
     8→Requirements for first working implementation. Each maps to roadmap phases.
     9→
    10→### Adapter Registry
    11→
    12→- [x] **AREG-01**: User can store a new adapter record with metadata (task_type, base_model_id, rank, file_path, file_hash, source) via AdapterRegistry.store()
    13→- [x] **AREG-02**: User can retrieve an adapter record by ID via AdapterRegistry.retrieve_by_id()
    14→- [x] **AREG-03**: User can query adapters by task_type via AdapterRegistry.query_by_task_type()
    15→- [x] **AREG-04**: User can list all non-archived adapters via AdapterRegistry.list_all()
    16→- [x] **AREG-05**: AdapterRegistry uses SQLite with WAL mode and engine-parameterized constructor for shared database access
    17→
    18→### Inference
    19→
    20→- [ ] **INF-01**: Abstract InferenceProvider interface with generate(), load_adapter(), unload_adapter(), list_adapters() methods
    21→- [ ] **INF-02**: VLLMProvider implementation with full LoRA hot-loading support (POST /v1/load_lora_adapter, /v1/unload_lora_adapter)
    22→- [ ] **INF-03**: OllamaProvider implementation for inference via Ollama API (LoRA support where Ollama supports it)
    23→- [ ] **INF-04**: Provider factory/registry for selecting backend by configuration (env var or config file)
    24→- [ ] **INF-05**: User can generate with a specific loaded adapter by passing adapter name as model parameter
    25→- [ ] **INF-06**: User can load multiple adapters simultaneously for composition (provider-dependent, graceful degradation)
    26→- [ ] **INF-07**: Per-step model/provider configuration — agent can use different models or providers for different steps (e.g., one model for generation, another for reflection)
    27→
    28→### Agent Loop
    29→
    30→- [ ] **AGENT-01**: generate_node calls InferenceProvider.generate() with task description and optional adapter, returning generated code (backend-agnostic)
    31→- [ ] **AGENT-02**: execute_node runs generated code in a sandboxed subprocess with timeout, returning stdout/stderr/exit_code/tests_passed
    32→- [ ] **AGENT-03**: reflect_node accumulates trajectory data (attempt count, code, results) without LLM call
    33→- [ ] **AGENT-04**: save_trajectory_node persists trajectory via record_trajectory() and sets outcome
    34→- [ ] **AGENT-05**: RuneState includes session_id field for trajectory persistence
    35→- [ ] **AGENT-06**: Agent loop closes end-to-end: generate → execute → reflect → retry/save with should_retry routing
    36→
    37→### Training Pipeline
    38→
    39→- [ ] **TRAIN-01**: User can record a coding trajectory as structured JSON via record_trajectory(session_id, steps, outcome)
    40→- [ ] **TRAIN-02**: User can convert trajectory data to SFT chat format via format_for_sft()
    41→- [ ] **TRAIN-03**: User can create a QLoRA config with NF4 quantization and bfloat16 compute dtype via build_qlora_config()
    42→- [ ] **TRAIN-04**: User can apply a LoRA adapter to a base model via apply_lora_adapter()
    43→- [ ] **TRAIN-05**: QLoRA training pipeline runs end-to-end: trajectory → SFT format → PEFT train → save safetensors → store in registry
    44→- [ ] **TRAIN-06**: training-svc exposes POST /train/lora endpoint with async background job tracking
    45→- [ ] **TRAIN-07**: training-svc pyproject.toml declares model-training as workspace dependency
    46→
    47→### Doc-to-LoRA Hypernetwork
    48→
    49→- [ ] **DTOL-01**: DocToLoraHypernetwork module exists in model-training lib with Perceiver-based architecture
    50→- [ ] **DTOL-02**: Hypernetwork generates rank-8 LoRA adapter weights from coding trajectory in a single forward pass (<1s)
    51→- [ ] **DTOL-03**: Generated adapters are compatible with vLLM dynamic LoRA loading (standard PEFT safetensors format)
    52→- [ ] **DTOL-04**: training-svc exposes POST /train/hypernetwork endpoint for Doc-to-LoRA training
    53→
    54→### Evaluation & Kill-Switch
    55→
    56→- [ ] **EVAL-01**: User can run a HumanEval subset benchmark via run_humaneval_subset()
    57→- [ ] **EVAL-02**: User can calculate Pass@k metrics via calculate_pass_at_k()
    58→- [ ] **EVAL-03**: Kill-switch gate compares baseline vs adapter-enhanced Pass@1 (5% improvement threshold)
    59→
    60→### Infrastructure
    61→
    62→- [ ] **INFRA-01**: lora-server Dockerfile uses vllm/vllm-openai:v0.16.0 base image (not python:3.12-slim)
    63→- [ ] **INFRA-02**: lora-server sets VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True environment variable
    64→- [ ] **INFRA-03**: docker-compose resolves port conflict (api-service and lora-server on different host ports)
    65→- [ ] **INFRA-04**: model-training pyproject.toml adds GPU dependencies (peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl, datasets) with TYPE_CHECKING guards
    66→- [ ] **INFRA-05**: All GPU imports deferred inside function bodies (not top-level) for CPU-only CI compatibility
    67→
    68→## Future Requirements
    69→
    70→Deferred to v6+. Tracked but not in current roadmap.
    71→
    72→### Evolution
    73→
    74→- **EVOL-01**: Evolution operator scores adapter fitness based on HumanEval pass rate
    75→- **EVOL-02**: Evolution operator performs adapter crossover via weight interpolation
    76→- **EVOL-03**: Evolution operator archives low-fitness adapters
    77→
    78→### Advanced Features
    79→
    80→- **ADV-01**: Embedding-based automatic adapter selection via vector similarity search
    81→- **ADV-02**: HITL UI with session monitoring, adapter library browser, trajectory viewer
    82→- **ADV-03**: Docker container isolation for code execution (replace subprocess sandbox)
    83→
    84→## Out of Scope
    85→
    86→Explicitly excluded. Documented to prevent scope creep.
    87→
    88→| Feature | Reason |
    89→|---------|--------|
    90→| Hardware validation phase | User deferred — will validate hardware separately |
    91→| Tensor parallelism (TP=2) | vLLM bug #21471 — corrupted output on PCIe GPUs without NVLink |
    92→| Adapter merging into base weights | Loses hot-swap composability; vLLM Punica kernels handle LoRA with near-zero overhead |
    93→| Cloud API inference | Hard constraint: local-first, no cloud dependencies for inference. Provider abstraction supports local backends (vLLM, Ollama) only |
    94→| Concurrent training + inference | CUDA OOM — must schedule sequentially |
    95→| Streaming responses in agent loop | Agent needs complete code, not token stream; add to API layer separately if needed |
    96→| Multi-tenant isolation | Single-user local system; session_id provides sufficient organization |
    97→| evolution-svc implementation | Depends on populated adapter library from multiple sessions; v6+ |

1→# Phase 19: Inference Provider Abstraction - Context
     2→
     3→**Gathered:** 2026-03-05
     4→**Status:** Ready for planning
     5→
     6→<domain>
     7→## Phase Boundary
     8→
     9→Provider-agnostic interface for code completions and LoRA adapter hot-loading. Two backends (vLLM, Ollama) selectable by configuration. Per-step model/provider config for the agent loop. Correctly configured lora-server Dockerfile and docker-compose port layout.
    10→
    11→</domain>
    12→
    13→<decisions>
    14→## Implementation Decisions
    15→
    16→### Provider Interface Shape
    17→- Replace existing module-level stubs (completion.py, adapter_loader.py) with an abstract `InferenceProvider` ABC — clean break, no legacy wrappers
    18→- `VLLMClient` in lora-server/ becomes `VLLMProvider` implementing the interface
    19→- All InferenceProvider methods are **async** (awaitable) — both backends are HTTP-based, async is natural
    20→- Keep the provider in `libs/inference/` — refactor in-place, no new workspace lib
    21→- `generate()` returns a **structured result** (dataclass/Pydantic model) with fields: text, model, adapter_id, token_count, finish_reason — agent loop needs metadata
    22→
    23→### Configuration & Backend Selection
    24→- Default provider selected by env var: `INFERENCE_PROVIDER=vllm|ollama` — consistent with `DATABASE_URL` and `VLLM_BASE_URL` patterns
    25→- Per-backend URL env vars: keep `VLLM_BASE_URL`, add `OLLAMA_BASE_URL` (default http://localhost:11434)
    26→- Per-step model/provider config via dict mapping at agent init: `{"generate": {"provider": "vllm", "model": "...", "adapter": "..."}, "reflect": {"provider": "ollama", "model": "..."}}`
    27→- Factory caches provider instances keyed by (provider_type, base_url) — avoids redundant HTTP clients when multiple steps use the same backend
    28→
    29→### Ollama Adapter Handling
    30→- `OllamaProvider.load_adapter()` raises `UnsupportedOperationError` — explicit failure, no silent no-ops
    31→- `OllamaProvider.generate()` fully works for base model completions — Ollama is useful for non-adapter steps (e.g., reflection)
    32→- Use httpx async client directly against Ollama's REST API — no extra ollama-python dependency
    33→- Use Ollama's **OpenAI-compatible endpoint** (`/v1/chat/completions`) — symmetrical with VLLMProvider, could even share the openai SDK
    34→
    35→### lora-server Docker & Port Layout
    36→- Dockerfile base image: `vllm/vllm-openai:v0.16.0` (replaces python:3.12-slim) — per INFRA-01
    37→- `VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True` env var set in Dockerfile — per INFRA-02
    38→- lora-server host port: **8100** (container port stays 8000, mapped to 8100 on host). Update `VLLM_BASE_URL` default to `http://localhost:8100/v1`
    39→- api-service stays on port 8000
    40→- Keep existing health sidecar on port 8001 — no change to health.py
    41→- Create minimal `docker-compose.yml` with api-service (8000) and lora-server (8100), shared SQLite volume mount — satisfies INFRA-03
    42→
    43→### Claude's Discretion
    44→- Exact InferenceProvider ABC method signatures beyond generate/load_adapter/unload_adapter/list_adapters
    45→- Internal error handling and retry logic within providers
    46→- Structured result dataclass field names and optional fields
    47→- Factory implementation pattern (module-level function vs class)
    48→- Test strategy for providers (mocked HTTP vs integration)
    49→
    50→</decisions>
    51→
    52→<specifics>
    53→## Specific Ideas
    54→
    55→- VLLMProvider uses the existing `openai` SDK (AsyncOpenAI) with custom base_url — established pattern from adapter_loader.py
    56→- OllamaProvider could also use AsyncOpenAI against Ollama's OpenAI-compat endpoint, making both providers nearly identical in HTTP layer
    57→- LoraServerConfig already has PP=2/TP=1 validation with `max_loras=8` — update `port` default and add `VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING` to config
    58→- Per-step config dict consumed by agent graph in Phase 20 — factory creates providers per step name
    59→
    60→</specifics>
    61→
    62→<code_context>
    63→## Existing Code Insights
    64→
    65→### Reusable Assets
    66→- `libs/inference/adapter_loader.py`: `get_vllm_client()` returns AsyncOpenAI — pattern reusable for VLLMProvider constructor
    67→- `services/lora-server/config.py`: `LoraServerConfig` dataclass with PP=2/TP=1 validation, YAML loading — update port default
    68→- `services/lora-server/vllm_client.py`: `VLLMClient` class — refactor into VLLMProvider
    69→- `openai` package already in workspace deps — reusable for both providers via OpenAI-compat endpoints
    70→- `httpx` already in workspace deps — available if Ollama needs raw HTTP
    71→
    72→### Established Patterns
    73→- Async HTTP clients via `openai.AsyncOpenAI` (adapter_loader.py, vllm_client.py)
    74→- Env var for service discovery (`VLLM_BASE_URL`, `DATABASE_URL`)
    75→- Dataclass configs with validation (`LoraServerConfig.__post_init__`)
    76→- Google-style docstrings, mypy strict, ruff
    77→- Module-level `__all__` exports in `__init__.py`
    78→
    79→### Integration Points
    80→- `services/lora-server/vllm_client.py` — becomes VLLMProvider or wraps it
    81→- `libs/inference/__init__.py` — re-exports need updating after refactor
    82→- `services/api-service/` — will use providers via factory (port 8000 unchanged)
    83→- Agent loop (Phase 20) — consumes per-step provider config dict
    84→
    85→</code_context>
